stories.title: Starmer to keep in touch with Trump after No 10
stories.summary: Prime Minister Keir Starmer has resigned from No. 10. At the NATO summit in Turkey, US President Trump wished him well during what was apparently a farewell meeting between the two leaders.

stories.title: Andy Burnham's path to becoming prime minister
stories.summary: Labour MPs have begun nominating candidates to succeed Sir Keir Starmer following his resignation. Andy Burnham is so far the only declared candidate in the Labour leadership contest.

stories.title: England to get bank holiday if team win World Cup, Starmer expected to announce
stories.summary: In one of his final acts as PM, Keir Starmer was expected to announce that England will receive a bank holiday if the national football team wins the 2026 World Cup — the first such victory since 1966.

topics.name: Parliament
topics.description: House of Commons, House of Lords, PMQs & Legislation

stories.title: Reeves to approve Farage's resignation from Parliament
stories.summary: Chancellor Rachel Reeves is set to approve Nigel Farage's resignation from the House of Commons, ignoring Ed Davey's request to block it until a parliamentary standards inquiry into the £5m gift concludes.

stories.title: PMQs dominated by Farage £5m NCA referral and Clacton by-election
stories.summary: Prime Minister's Questions saw heated exchanges over Nigel Farage's resignation from Parliament and the referral of his £5m gift to the National Crime Agency. Labour called on Farage to cooperate with authorities.

topics.name: Labour Party
topics.description: Labour Party news, policy & internal politics

stories.title: Burnham promises Labour MPs he will not use party discipline to stifle debate
stories.summary: PM-in-waiting Andy Burnham has pledged to address backbench frustrations that built up under Keir Starmer's tight management style, vowing not to use the whipping system to suppress internal party debate.

stories.title: Report to National Crime Agency of £5m Farage gift is 'deeply serious'
stories.summary: Labour's party chair described the NCA referral over Nigel Farage's £5m gift as 'deeply serious' and called on the Reform UK leader to cooperate with authorities rather than use the Clacton by-election as a distraction.

stories.title: Labour vows to boycott 'circus' Clacton by-election
stories.summary: Labour confirmed it will not field a candidate in the Clacton by-election triggered by Farage's resignation, branding it a political stunt designed to distract from ongoing financial scrutiny.

stories.title: Farage's rivals rule out standing in Clacton by-election
stories.summary: The Conservative Party joined other major parties in confirming they will not contest the Clacton by-election. Reform UK's Farage says he wants an 'establishment versus the people' contest, but the Conservatives and others have dismissed it as a circus.

stories.title: Jackdaw boss warns of winter fuel shortages if gas field not approved
stories.summary: The boss of Adura, operator of the Jackdaw gas field, warned the UK government must urgently approve North Sea production to avoid domestic supply shortages this winter — an issue the Conservative opposition has been pressing the government on.

stories.title: Lib Dems tell Farage 'the game is up' as they boycott Clacton
stories.summary: The Liberal Democrats joined the political boycott of the Clacton by-election, with the party telling Farage that 'the game is up' amid the NCA investigation into his £5m financial gift.

stories.title: Ed Davey's request to block Farage's Commons resignation rejected
stories.summary: Chancellor Reeves rejected Lib Dem leader Ed Davey's call to block Nigel Farage's resignation from the House of Commons until the parliamentary standards inquiry into his £5m gift has concluded.

stories.title: Farage resigns as Clacton MP to trigger by-election over £5m gift scandal
stories.summary: Nigel Farage has resigned as MP for Clacton to force a by-election, framing it as 'the people versus the establishment' vote following the referral of a £5m gift he received to the National Crime Agency.

stories.title: The untold story of why Reform figures face NCA scrutiny
stories.summary: An exclusive investigation reveals the details behind financial transactions made by Reform UK figures that banks flagged to the National Crime Agency, providing background on the emerging scandal.

stories.title: Nigel Farage denies his Clacton resignation is a publicity stunt
stories.summary: In a BBC interview, Farage defended his decision to resign and stand again, saying it is 'only fair' that the electorate judges him on his financial record rather than Parliament.

stories.title: Scottish parties react to Clacton by-election boycott
stories.summary: Malcolm Offord, a Reform UK figure in Scotland, claimed other parties were missing a chance to 'knock Farage out of the ring' by boycotting the Clacton by-election, sparking debate across Scottish political circles.

topics.name: Cabinet Office
topics.description: Government reshuffles, senior appointments & cross-department policy

stories.title: CAC expanded with 23 new members to support Employment Rights Act
stories.summary: The Central Arbitration Committee has recruited 23 new members to support implementation of changes introduced by the Employment Rights Act 2025, with new appointments starting on 8 July 2026.

topics.name: Elections & By-elections
topics.description: Upcoming or recent election results & polls

stories.title: Clacton by-election likely to take place on 6 August
stories.summary: Reform UK is asking for the Clacton by-election to be held on 6 August following Farage's resignation. All major parties have announced a boycott, with Count Binface and Laurence Fox among the few declared candidates.

stories.title: Wednesday briefing: How Farage's by-election gambit may already have backfired
stories.summary: Analysis of how Reform UK's decision to resign and trigger a by-election over financial scandal may have already backfired, with the NCA investigation overshadowing the 'people v establishment' narrative.

stories.title: Count Binface on Clacton byelection: likely to be two-man race against Farage
stories.summary: Parody candidate Count Binface is expected to be Reform leader's only challenger for the Clacton seat, after all serious political parties confirmed they will boycott the contest.

stories.title: Prisons Minister Lord Timpson on fostering, business and prison reform
stories.summary: Lord Timpson, the Prisons Minister, spoke to Lord Forsyth of Drumlean about fostering, business leadership and prison reform in the latest episode of Lord Speaker's Corner podcast.

stories.title: Lords committee: Peter Mandelson appointment as US Ambassador 'disastrous'
stories.summary: A House of Commons foreign affairs committee report found the decision to appoint Peter Mandelson as British Ambassador to the United States has been 'nothing short of disastrous', raising questions about the appointments process.
